context: =  OROPHARYNGEAL IMPAIRMENT = SWALLOWING DYSFUNCTION Prevalence:   22% in adult primary care population, M÷F = 1÷4; 13% in general population; in up to 50% of nursing home residents Predisposed:   gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), stroke, Alzheimer disease, multiple sclerosis, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, Parkinson disease Neuromuscular disorders and structural abnormalities increase with age. Look for a cause of dysphagia in elderly patients. Cause: Wallenberg syndrome (lateral medullary syndrome) ◊  29-64% of stroke patients become dysphagic; 20% die from aspiration pneumonia ›  esop
